title: Emergence — Residual Neuromuscular Blockade
description: An intubated patient on volatile maintenance approaches emergence with administratively constructed residual rocuronium blockade.
learningObjectives: Stop active anesthetic delivery before extubation
Assess and reverse residual blockade through the single NMB state
Confirm adequate spontaneous ventilation before extubation

debrief.summary: The patient begins learner time already intubated, mechanically ventilated, receiving volatile anesthesia, and below a TOF ratio of 0.90 through the same rocuronium state used by drug and reversal logic.
teachingPoints: Administrative preconditioning is not learner drug or TOF evidence.
TOF recovery and spontaneous ventilation remain separate extubation assessments.
The first learner trace is rebased to t=0 after fixed-step equilibration.
